**Adult cutoff:** Patients born on or before `2008-06-18` (≥18 years as of 2026-06-18)

---

### 🫀 Heart Failure with Preserved EF (HFpEF) — Diastolic HF Conditions

```
Condition?code=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|I50.30,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|I50.31,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|I50.32,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|I50.33,http://snomed.info/sct|446221000&subject:Patient.birthdate=le2008-06-18&_include=Condition:subject
```

### 🫀 Heart Failure with Reduced EF (HFrEF) — Systolic HF Conditions

```
Condition?code=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|I50.20,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|I50.21,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|I50.22,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|I50.23,http://snomed.info/sct|703272007&subject:Patient.birthdate=le2008-06-18&_include=Condition:subject
```

---

### 📊 Echocardiogram EF Measurements — Preserved (EF > 50%)

```

```

### 📊 Echocardiogram EF Measurements — Reduced (EF < 50%)

Observation?code=http://loinc.org|8806-2,http://loinc.org|10230-1,http://loinc.org|77889-7&value-quantity=lt50||%25&category=http://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem/observation-category|imaging&subject:Patient.birthdate=le2008-06-18&_include=Observation:subject
```

---

### 📋 Code Reference

| Query | Code System | Codes Used |
|---|---|---|
| HFpEF Conditions | ICD-10-CM | `I50.30–I50.33` (diastolic HF: unspecified/acute/chronic/acute-on-chronic) |
| HFpEF Conditions | SNOMED CT | `446221000` (HF with preserved EF) |

```

### Design rationale

| Dimension | Decision |
|---|---|
| **Code systems** | Server confirmed dual SNOMED CT + ICD-10-CM coding on every Condition; both used in all Condition queries |
| **HFpEF codes** | VSAC OID `2.16.840.1.113762.1.4.1222.596` expanded → 15 SNOMED codes (diastolic HF, HF-with-normal-EF, staging) + ICD-10-CM `I50.30–I50.33` |
| **HFrEF codes** | VSAC OID `2.16.840.1.113762.1.4.1222.578` expanded → 17 SNOMED codes (systolic HF, HFrEF by etiology, staging) + ICD-10-CM `I50.20–I50.23` |
| **Echo EF LOINC codes** | AHA VSAC value set OID `2.16.840.1.113883.3.526.2.1238` expanded (50 codes); filtered to 21 **ultrasound/echocardiogram-specific** LOINC codes (excluded angiogram, CT, MRI, nuclear); `8806-2` confirmed present on server |
| **EF value thresholds** | `value-quantity=gt50||%` for HFpEF (preserved, >50%); `value-quantity=lt50||%` for HFrEF (reduced, <50%) |
| **Adult filter** | `patient.birthdate=le2008-05-21` (≥18 y/o as of 2026-05-21) applied to all 4 queries |
| **Runner instruction** | Join Queries 1+3 by patient for HFpEF cohort; join Queries 2+4 by patient for HFrEF cohort |
2-naive.prompt_text: Find all adult patients diagnosed with heart failure, including those with preserved ejection fraction (greater than 50%) and those with reduced ejection fraction (less than 50%), along with their echocardiogram ejection fraction measurements.

2-broad.raw_response:
All four queries return plausible, non-zero counts: **38 HFpEF conditions**, **185 HFrEF conditions**, **518 EF > 50% observations**, and **132 EF < 50% observations** (650 total echo EF measurements — matching our earlier baseline count ✓). Here is the complete verified query set:

---

### 📋 Query Design Summary

| Layer | Resource | Codes Used | Count |
|---|---|---|---|
| **HFpEF Dx (adults)** | Condition | SNOMED preserved EF subtypes + ICD-10 I50.3x | 38 |
| **HFrEF Dx (adults)** | Condition | SNOMED reduced EF subtypes + ICD-10 I50.2x | 185 |
| **Echo EF > 50%** (HFpEF measurements) | Observation | LOINC echo EF panel (8806-2, 8807-0, 10230-1, 18043-0, 18047-1, 77892-8, 79991-6) | 518 |
| **Echo EF < 50%** (HFrEF measurements) | Observation | Same LOINC echo EF panel | 132 |

**Code strategy:**
- **Server confirmed** triple coding: SNOMED CT + ICD-10-CM + ICD-9-CM on Condition resources
- **HFpEF** maps to ICD-10 `I50.3x` (diastolic HF) family + 12 SNOMED subtypes from VSAC "Chronic Heart Failure With Preserved Ejection Fraction" value set
- **HFrEF** maps to ICD-10 `I50.2x` (systolic HF) family + 15 SNOMED subtypes from VSAC "Chronic Heart Failure With Reduced Ejection Fraction" value set
- **Adults** enforced via `patient.birthdate=le2008-05-21` (≥18 years as of today)
- **Echo EF** confirmed on server as LOINC `8806-2`; expanded with 6 additional echo-specific LOINC codes from the AHA "Ejection Fraction" VSAC value set
- Union of all 4 queries client-side yields the full cohort with measurements

# FHIR Queries: Heart Failure Population for GDMT Initiative

Below are **5 queries across 3 clinical approaches**, designed to maximize capture of your HF population. Extract the `subject` (Patient reference) from each result set and union them to build your complete cohort.

---

## APPROACH 1 — Heart Failure Diagnosis Codes

### Query 1a — ICD-10-CM (Explicit I50.x + Hypertensive HF Variants)

Covers: HFrEF (I50.2x), HFpEF (I50.3x), combined (I50.4x), unspecified (I50.9), LV failure (I50.1), hypertensive HF (I11.0, I13.0, I13.2)

```
Condition?code=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|I50.1,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|I50.9,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|I50.20,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|I50.21,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|I50.22,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|I50.23,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|I50.30,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|I50.31,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|I50.32,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|I50.33,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|I50.40,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|I50.41,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|I50.42,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|I50.43,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|I11.0,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|I13.0,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|I13.2&clinical-status=active&_include=Condition:subject
```

> **Code map:**
> | Code | Description |
> |------|-------------|
> | I50.1 | Left ventricular failure |
> | I50.20–23 | Systolic HF (HFrEF) — unspecified, acute, chronic, acute-on-chronic |
> | I50.30–33 | Diastolic HF (HFpEF) — unspecified, acute, chronic, acute-on-chronic |
> | I50.40–43 | Combined systolic/diastolic HF |
> | I50.9 | Heart failure, unspecified (CHF) |
> | I11.0 | Hypertensive heart disease with HF |
> | I13.0 / I13.2 | Hypertensive heart + CKD with HF |

---

### Query 1b — SNOMED CT (Hierarchical Expansion via `:below`)

Catches HFrEF, HFpEF, HFmrEF, CHF, and all descendant concepts under **84114007 (Heart failure)** in a single query. Requires server support for the `:below` modifier (SNOMED hierarchy traversal).

```
Condition?code:below=http://snomed.info/sct|84114007&clinical-status=active&_include=Condition:subject
```

> **Key SNOMED descendants captured:**
> | SNOMED Code | Description |
> |-------------|-------------|
> | 84114007 | Heart failure (root concept) |
> | 48447003 | Chronic heart failure |
> | 417996009 | Systolic heart failure (HFrEF) |
> | 418304008 | Diastolic heart failure (HFpEF) |
> | 446221000 | HF with normal EF (HFpEF equivalent) |
> | 233924009 | HF with reduced EF |

---

## APPROACH 2 — Active GDMT Medication Prescriptions

### Query 2 — Sacubitril/Valsartan (ARNi) · Carvedilol · Spironolactone · Empagliflozin · Furosemide

```
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|1599538,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|20352,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|9997,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|1545653,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|4603&status=active&_include=MedicationRequest:subject
```

> **RxNorm code map:**
> | RxNorm CUI | Drug | GDMT Class |
> |------------|------|------------|
> | 1599538 | Sacubitril (ingredient unique to ARNi/Entresto) | ARNi |
> | 20352 | Carvedilol | Beta-blocker |
> | 9997 | Spironolactone | MRA |
> | 1545653 | Empagliflozin | SGLT2 inhibitor |
> | 4603 | Furosemide | Loop diuretic |

> ⚠️ **Clinical Notes:**
> - **Sacubitril** (1599538) is used as the ARNi proxy since it is never prescribed without valsartan — this avoids false positives from patients on valsartan alone for HTN.
> - **Spironolactone** and **Empagliflozin** are also prescribed for non-HF indications (hyperaldosteronism, diabetes). Treat these patients as *candidate screens*, not confirmed HF.
> - If your EHR stores medications as references rather than inline codes, supplement with: `MedicationRequest?medication.code=...` using chained search.

---

## APPROACH 3 — Echocardiogram Ejection Fraction < 50%

### Query 3a — EF Stored as Percentage (e.g., value = 40, unit = %)

```
Observation?code=http://loinc.org|8806-2,http://loinc.org|10230-1,http://loinc.org|18043-0,http://loinc.org|77889-0&value-quantity=lt50|http://unitsofmeasure.org|%25&_include=Observation:subject
```

### Query 3b — EF Stored as Decimal Ratio (e.g., value = 0.40, unit = 1)

```
Observation?code=http://loinc.org|8806-2,http://loinc.org|10230-1,http://loinc.org|18043-0,http://loinc.org|77889-0&value-quantity=lt0.5|http://unitsofmeasure.org|1&_include=Observation:subject
```

> **LOINC code map:**
> | LOINC Code | Description |
> |------------|-------------|
> | 8806-2 | LV Ejection Fraction by 2D Echo |
> | 10230-1 | LV Ejection Fraction by Ultrasound |
> | 18043-0 | LV Ejection Fraction (general) |
> | 77889-0 | LV EF by US 2D + Modified Simpson Method |

> ⚠️ **Run both 3a and 3b** — storage conventions vary by EHR/cardiology platform. Union the patient IDs from both result sets.

---

## Union Logic: Building the Final Patient Cohort

```
┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  STEP 1: Execute all 5 queries                                    │
│  STEP 2: From each FHIR Bundle, extract                           │
│          Condition.subject / MedicationRequest.subject /          │
│          Observation.subject → collect Patient IDs               │
│  STEP 3: UNION all Patient ID sets (deduplicate)                  │
│  STEP 4: Optional — for each patient, note which signals fired:  │
│          [DX] [MED] [ECHO] for downstream GDMT gap analysis      │
└───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

**Suggested enrichment after cohort identification:**
- Pull current LVEF values: `Observation?subject=[patientId]&code=http://loinc.org|8806-2&_sort=-date&_count=1`
- Pull current GDMT medications: `MedicationRequest?subject=[patientId]&status=active&code=...` (reuse Query 2 codes)
- Pull labs (BMP, BNP/NT-proBNP): `Observation?subject=[patientId]&code=http://loinc.org|33762-6,http://loinc.org|71425-3`

---

**Additional caveats to be aware of:**
1. `clinical-status=active` on Condition may exclude historical diagnoses — remove this filter if your documentation practices are inconsistent.
2. Add `_count=500&_total=accurate` for pagination on large populations.
3. The `%25` in Query 3a is the URL-encoded form of the `%` UCUM unit — confirm your REST client handles encoding correctly.
4. If your server doesn't support `:below` for SNOMED (Query 1b), fall back to an explicit SNOMED code list or a ValueSet reference (`code:in=http://your-server/ValueSet/heart-failure-snomed`).
1-naive.prompt_text:
I need to identify my complete heart failure patient population for a guideline-directed medical therapy (GDMT) optimization initiative. From clinical experience, I know HF patients are documented in different ways: some have a formal diagnosis code (CHF, HFrEF, HFpEF), some are on classic HF medications (loop diuretics, ARNi, SGLT2 inhibitors, MRAs) without a formal HF diagnosis, and some have a documented reduced ejection fraction on echo.

Please run multiple FHIR queries to capture the full population:
1. Patients with any heart failure diagnosis (CHF, HFrEF, HFpEF, or general heart failure)
2. Patients prescribed HF medications (sacubitril/valsartan, carvedilol, spironolactone, empagliflozin, furosemide)
3. Patients with echocardiogram ejection fraction measurements below 50 percent

For each query, return the matching resources. I want the union of all patients found across these three approaches.

1-broad.raw_response:
### Query 2A · Sacubitril/Valsartan — ARNi (Entresto) · All Dose Strengths (completed)

```
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|1656328,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|1656340,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|1656348,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|1656354&status=active&_include=MedicationRequest:patient
```

| RxNorm CUI | Description |
|---|---|
| 1656328 | Sacubitril / valsartan (combination ingredient concept) |
| 1656340 | Entresto 24 mg / 26 mg tablet |
| 1656348 | Entresto 49 mg / 51 mg tablet |
| 1656354 | Entresto 97 mg / 103 mg tablet |

---

### Query 2B · Carvedilol — Beta-Blocker (BB)
> Ingredient-level code captures all formulations and strengths (3.125 mg, 6.25 mg, 12.5 mg, 25 mg).

```
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|20352&status=active&_include=MedicationRequest:patient
```

---

### Query 2C · Spironolactone — Mineralocorticoid Receptor Antagonist (MRA)
> Captures use in both HFrEF (evidence-based GDMT dose) and HFpEF (TOPCAT evidence). Consider adding eplerenone (RxNorm `298869`) as a supplemental MRA query.

```
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|9997&status=active&_include=MedicationRequest:patient
```

---

### Query 2D · Empagliflozin — SGLT2 Inhibitor
> Covers EMPEROR-Reduced/Preserved indication. Consider also querying dapagliflozin (RxNorm `2200648`) for DAPA-HF patients.

```
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|1545653&status=active&_include=MedicationRequest:patient
```

---

### Query 2E · Furosemide — Loop Diuretic
> High-sensitivity catch-all: furosemide is nearly universal in symptomatic HF patients even without a formal diagnosis code. May yield false positives (e.g., hypertension, CKD) — use for population enrichment, not confirmation.

```
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|4603&status=active&_include=MedicationRequest:patient
```

---

### Query 2F · MedicationStatement — All HF Medications Combined
> Catches medications documented outside the prescribing workflow (patient-reported, care transitions, external records). Covers all five drugs in a single query.

```
MedicationStatement?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|1656328,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|1656340,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|1656348,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|1656354,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|20352,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|9997,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|1545653,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|4603&status=active&_include=MedicationStatement:patient
```

---

## 📊 GROUP 3 — Echocardiogram Ejection Fraction < 50% (Observation)

> Two queries are required because EF is stored in two different unit conventions depending on the system or vendor: **percentage (0–100%)** or **decimal ratio (0.0–1.0)**. Run both to avoid missing patients.

---

### Query 3A · LVEF < 50% — Percentage Scale (most common: stored as e.g., "45 %")
> Covers standard 2D echo, biplane Simpson's, and 3D volumetric EF measurements. `status=final` excludes preliminary/amended reports.

```
Observation?code=http://loinc.org|10230-1,http://loinc.org|8834-4,http://loinc.org|79989-7&value-quantity=lt50|http://unitsofmeasure.org|%25&status=final&_include=Observation:patient
```

---

### Query 3B · LVEF < 0.50 — Decimal Ratio Scale (alternate: stored as e.g., "0.45")
> Same LOINC codes; catches systems that store the value as a unitless fraction rather than a percentage.

```
Observation?code=http://loinc.org|10230-1,http://loinc.org|8834-4,http://loinc.org|79989-7&value-quantity=lt0.50|http://unitsofmeasure.org|1&status=final&_include=Observation:patient
```

| LOINC Code | Description |
|---|---|
| 10230-1 | Left ventricular Ejection fraction (general) |
| 8834-4 | Left ventricular Ejection fraction (quantitative) |
| 79989-7 | Left ventricular Ejection fraction by 3D echo |

---

## 📋 Complete Query Reference Summary

| # | Group | Resource | Target | Key Parameter |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1A | Diagnoses | `Condition` | ICD-10-CM I50.1–I50.9 (23 codes) | `clinical-status=active` |
| 1B | Diagnoses | `Condition` | SNOMED CT HF (10 concepts) | `clinical-status=active` |
| 2A | Medications | `MedicationRequest` | Sacubitril/Valsartan (4 RxNorm) | `status=active` |
| 2B | Medications | `MedicationRequest` | Carvedilol | `status=active` |
| 2C | Medications | `MedicationRequest` | Spironolactone | `status=active` |
| 2D | Medications | `MedicationRequest` | Empagliflozin | `status=active` |
| 2E | Medications | `MedicationRequest` | Furosemide | `status=active` |
| 2F | Medications | `MedicationStatement` | All 5 HF drugs combined | `status=active` |
| 3A | Echo EF | `Observation` | LVEF < 50% (percentage) | `value-quantity=lt50\|...\|%25` |
| 3B | Echo EF | `Observation` | LVEF < 0.50 (ratio) | `value-quantity=lt0.50\|...\|1` |

---

## ⚠️ Implementation Notes

| Topic | Guidance |
|---|---|
| **Union deduplication** | Extract `subject.reference` (Patient ID) from all returned resources across all 10 queries; deduplicate to produce the final cohort list |
| **`_include` behavior** | `_include=Condition:patient` etc. returns full `Patient` resources in the same Bundle — enables single-pass patient data retrieval |
| **clinical-status gaps** | Many EHRs do not reliably populate `Condition.clinicalStatus`; if Query 1A/1B return unexpectedly low counts, remove `clinical-status=active` and filter manually |
| **Furosemide specificity** | Query 2E is high-sensitivity/low-specificity — furosemide is also used for HTN and CKD; treat these results as candidates requiring clinical confirmation |
| **Hierarchical SNOMED** | If your FHIR server supports the `code:below` modifier (e.g., HAPI FHIR), replace Query 1B with: `Condition?code:below=http://snomed.info/sct\|84114007` |
| **Pagination** | Large populations will require following `Bundle.link[relation=next]` URLs; add `&_count=200` to increase page size |
| **EF unit ambiguity** | Always run both 3A and 3B — mismatched unit conventions are a common cause of missed echo results |
| **Eplerenone / Dapagliflozin** | Consider supplemental queries for RxNorm `298869` (eplerenone) and `2200648` (dapagliflozin) to fully capture MRA and SGLT2i classes |

> **Note:** Queries use RxNorm ingredient-level codes for maximum recall across formulations. `status=active` limits to current prescriptions. Also consider querying `MedicationStatement` and `MedicationDispense` if your EHR populates those resources.

---

```

| LOINC Code | Description |

| 8806-2 | Left ventricular Ejection fraction by 2D echo *(primary)* |
| 18043-0 | Left ventricular Ejection fraction (general/calculated) |
| 77889-2 | Left ventricular Ejection fraction by 2D echo (biplane method) |
| 55712-7 | Ejection fraction — visual estimate |

**Value filter explanation:**
```
value-quantity=lt50|http://unitsofmeasure.org|%25
               ────  ─────────────────────────  ───
               <50   UCUM system URI             % unit
```

> **Threshold note:** EF < 50% captures HFrEF (EF ≤40%) and HFmrEF (41–49%). To narrow exclusively to HFrEF, change to `lt40`. To include borderline cases, use `lt55`.

## 🔗 Query Summary Table

| # | Resource | Approach | Key Signal |
|---|----------|----------|------------|
| 1A | `Condition` | ICD-10 I50.2x | HFrEF (systolic) |

| 1C | `Condition` | ICD-10 I50.1/I50.4x/I50.9 | CHF / combined / unspecified |
| 1D | `Condition` | SNOMED CT | HF in SNOMED-coded systems |
| 2A | `MedicationRequest` | RxNorm 1656354 | ARNi (sacubitril/valsartan) |
| 2B | `MedicationRequest` | RxNorm 20352 | Beta-blocker (carvedilol) |
| 2C | `MedicationRequest` | RxNorm 9997 | MRA (spironolactone) |
| 2D | `MedicationRequest` | RxNorm 1545653 | SGLT2i (empagliflozin) |
| 2E | `MedicationRequest` | RxNorm 4603 | Loop diuretic (furosemide) |
| 3A | `Observation` | LOINC EF codes, value <50% | Reduced EF on echo |

---

## ⚠️ Implementation Notes

1. **Deduplication:** Collect all `subject` (Patient) references from all 10 result bundles. The union set = your HF population. A patient appearing in multiple queries appears only once in the cohort.

2. **Pagination:** For large populations, add `&_count=200&_getpagesoffset=0` and iterate with the `next` link in the returned Bundle.

3. **False positives in Query 2C/2E:** Spironolactone (also for hyperaldosteronism, cirrhosis) and furosemide (also for HTN, edema) have non-HF indications — manually review or cross-reference with absence of a competing diagnosis.

4. **Echo code variation:** LOINC codes for EF vary significantly by echo system vendor and interfa

From clinical experience, I know HF patients are documented in different ways: some have a formal diagnosis code (CHF, HFrEF, HFpEF), some are on classic HF medications (loop diuretics, ARNi, SGLT2 inhibitors, MRAs) without a formal HF diagnosis, and some have a documented reduced ejection fraction on echo.

Please run multiple FHIR queries to capture the full population:
1. Patients with any heart failure diagnosis (CHF, HFrEF, HFpEF, or general heart failure)
2. Patients prescribed HF medications (sacubitril/valsartan, carvedilol, spironolactone, empagliflozin, furosemide)
3. Patients with echocardiogram ejection fraction measurements below 50 percent

For each query, return the matching resources. I want the union of all patients found across these three approaches.

3-broad.raw_response:
All queries are validated. Here's a comprehensive summary of findings and the final FHIR queries:

---

## 🔍 Methodology Summary

**Playbooks applied:** #8 (Multi-system code lists), #12 (Cohort = OR union), #7 (Threshold-based EF)

### What the server uses:
| Resource | Code System(s) confirmed |
|---|---|
| `Condition` | SNOMED CT + ICD-10-CM + ICD-9-CM (often all three per resource) |
| `MedicationRequest` | RxNorm (ingredient-level) |
| `Observation` | LOINC 8806-2 for LV EF by 2D echo |

---

| `417996009` | SNOMED | Systolic heart failure |

| `I50.20–23` | ICD-10 | Systolic HF (all acuity variants) |
| `I50.30–33` | ICD-10 | Diastolic HF (all acuity variants) |
| `I50.40–43` | ICD-10 | Combined systolic+diastolic HF |
| `I50.9` | ICD-10 | HF unspecified |
| `428.0, 428.1, 428.20–23, 428.30–33, 428.9` | ICD-9 | Legacy HF codes |

## Query 3 — Reduced Ejection Fraction by Echo (Observation)

Uses 7 LOINC EF codes (AHA value set 2.16.840.1.113883.3.526.2.1238) covering 2D echo, biplane, modified Simpson, M-mode, and 3D methods — server primarily records `8806-2`.

### 💡 Important Notes for GDMT Optimization Use

1. **Medication query is a superset**: 465 medication records > 299 diagnosis records, confirming your clinical intuition that many HF patients on loop diuretics, spironolactone, or SGLT2i lack a formal coded HF diagnosis. These are high-priority patients to review.

2. **EF < 50% adds an independent signal**: 132 echo observations — deduplicate against the diagnosis cohort to identify patients with documented reduced EF who may be candidates for intensified HFrEF GDMT (beta-blocker titration, ARNi up-titration).

3. **ICD-9 did not add net-new patients** on this server — all ICD-9-coded Condition resources also carry ICD-10 codes — but they are included for completeness and future-proofing.

4. **Union deduplication**: Your harness should merge by `Patient.id` across all three resource sets to produce the final unduplicated cohort.

| 1 | SNOMED CT | `84114007` | Heart failure (broad clinical concept) |
| 2 | ICD-10-CM | `I50.9` | Heart failure, unspecified |

```

**Query Breakdown:**

| SNOMED Code | Display | Phenotype |
|---|---|---|
| `84114007` | Heart failure | Parent / General |
| `42343007` | Congestive heart failure | General |
| `88805009` | Chronic congestive heart failure | General |
| `703272007` | Heart failure with reduced ejection fraction | HFrEF |
| `446221000` | Heart failure with normal ejection fraction | HFpEF |

**Notes & Recommendations:**

- **Comma-separated codes** in the `code` parameter apply **OR logic**, returning Conditions matching any of the five codes in a single round trip.
- **Subsumption (`above`/`below`):** If your FHIR server supports SNOMED hierarchy traversal, you could alternatively use `Condition?code:below=http://snomed.info/sct|84114007` to capture *all* descendants of the parent concept automatically — though explicit codes are more portable and predictable across servers.
- **Active records only:** Consider appending `&clinical-status=active` if you only want current diagnoses and not resolved/historical entries.
- **Include patient context:** Add `&_include=Condition:patient` to pull associated Patient resources in the same bundle if building a cohort list.
- **ICD-10-CM parity:** Many EHRs code heart failure under ICD-10 (e.g., `I50.9`, `I50.20`, `I50.30`) — consider a parallel query against those codes if your system stores diagnoses in ICD-10 rather than SNOMED.

## FHIR Queries: Low Ejection Fraction on Echo

"Low" ejection fraction is clinically defined as **< 40%** (reduced EF / HFrEF). The following queries cover the most common LOINC codes for LVEF measured by echocardiogram:

```
Observation?code=http://loinc.org|8806-2&value-quantity=lt40|http://unitsofmeasure.org|%25&status=final

Observation?code=http://loinc.org|10230-1&value-quantity=lt40|http://unitsofmeasure.org|%25&status=final

Observation?code=http://loinc.org|18043-0&value-quantity=lt40|http://unitsofmeasure.org|%25&status=final

Observation?code=http://loinc.org|77889-4&value-quantity=lt40|http://unitsofmeasure.org|%25&status=final
```

---

### LOINC Code Reference

| LOINC Code | Description |
|---|---|
| `8806-2` | Left ventricular Ejection fraction by 2D echo |
| `10230-1` | Left ventricular Ejection fraction by US |
| `18043-0` | Left ventricular Ejection fraction (general) |
| `77889-4` | Left ventricular Ejection fraction by 2D echo (biplane) |

### Notes
- **< 40%** = Reduced EF (HFrEF) — most clinical guideline threshold for "low"
- Adjust threshold to **< 50%** (`lt50`) if capturing mildly reduced EF (HFmrEF) as well
- Add `&_include=Observation:patient` to retrieve associated Patient resources
- Add `&patient=[id]` to scope to a specific patient

1-expert.raw_response:
```
Observation?code=http://loinc.org|10230-1&value-quantity=lt50||%25
```

**Query breakdown:**

| Component | Value | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| **Resource** | `Observation` | Echocardiographic measurements are stored as Observations |
| **LOINC code** | `10230-1` | "Left ventricular Ejection fraction by 2D echo" — the standard LOINC for 2D echo-derived EF |
| **Comparator** | `lt` (less than) | Strictly below the 50% HFrEF threshold; `le` (≤) would incorrectly include borderline-normal EF of exactly 50% |
| **Value** | `50` | PheKB HFrEF cutoff |
| **Unit (UCUM)** | `%25` | URL-encoded `%` (percent); no specific coding system prefix needed for UCUM inline units |

**Clinical context notes:**
- `10230-1` is distinct from codes for EF by other modalities (e.g., nuclear medicine, MRI) — important to not conflate methods
- The strict `lt50` filter aligns with PheKB's EF < 50% criterion; using `le` would also capture EF = 50%, which PheKB classifies as HFmrEF (mid-range)
- To maximize recall, you may also wish to add `10230-1` sibling codes (e.g., `LOINC|8806-2` — "Left ventricular Ejection fraction by US") in a combined query using `_list` or multiple `code=` values with `,` (OR semantics) depending on your EHR's coding practice

### 🔎 Gap Analysis Logic
- **Step 1** identifies the HF GDMT-treated population (465 medication orders)
- **Step 2** identifies documented HF patients to exclude (299 condition records)
- **Documentation gap** = patients appearing in Step 1 **but not** Step 2

> **Note:** The Azure FHIR server (R4, v4.0.786) does not expose `_has` or `:not` modifiers for cross-resource negation, so the gap must be computed client-side as the set-difference of patient references between the two result sets.
